name: 1St Liberty
description_short: 1st Liberty Federal Credit Union is a Montana-based credit union serving six counties with online/mobile banking, auto loans, mortgages, and savings products.
description_long:
1st Liberty Federal Credit Union operates as a community-chartered financial institution in Montana, primarily serving the counties of Cascade, Pondera, Teton, Chouteau, and Glacier. The credit union has been established long enough to celebrate a 70th anniversary, indicating stability and longevity in the market. They operate multiple physical branches in Great Falls, Montana, with drive-thru facilities and maintain a customer service line at 1-800-824-0585.

1st Liberty offers a comprehensive suite of banking services including online and mobile banking platforms, auto loans with rates as low as 3.99% APR for 2021 and newer vehicles, mortgage loans starting at 4.74% APR, home equity lines of credit, Visa credit cards, personal signature loans, share certificates, and retirement accounts. They provide standard banking conveniences such as ATM access, calculators, and loan rate tools on their website. The credit union recently upgraded their digital banking platform as of April 7, requiring members to redownload their mobile app and re-enroll in online services.

As a federally chartered credit union, 1st Liberty distinguishes itself through membership eligibility based on community ties rather than employment. Membership is open to anyone who lives, works, attends school, worships, volunteers, or participates in associations within their designated service counties. This community-focused approach aligns with the credit union model of member-owned, not-for-profit banking. Their competitive auto loan rates and featured mortgage rates suggest they actively compete on pricing.

1st Liberty serves a specific geographic market limited to central Montana counties. While they offer all standard banking products, they are not a national institution and lack the technological sophistication or product breadth of larger national banks or online-only neobanks. Recent digital banking system changes indicate they may lag behind competitors in seamless technology implementation. For Montana residents within their service area seeking traditional credit union banking with reasonable rates, they represent a stable local option.
services: Online banking with secure login and account management
Mobile banking app (rebranded with white background and green logo as of April 2025)
Auto loans with tiered rates by vehicle age (3.99%-6.99% APR)
First and second mortgage lending (4.74%-5.74% APR)
Home equity lines of credit (7.00% variable APR)
Personal signature loans (8.70% APR for 70th anniversary special)
Share certificates and savings accounts (up to 3.30% APY)
ATM network access and locator service
pros: Federally chartered credit union offering member-owned, not-for-profit banking structure
Auto loan rates as low as 3.99% APR for 2021 and newer vehicles
Mortgage rates as low as 4.74% APR for first mortgages
Community-based membership eligibility including employment, residence, school attendance, and volunteer work in service counties
Multiple physical branch locations in Great Falls with drive-thru service and extended hours (8am opening at some locations)
Online and mobile banking platforms with Zelle and BillPay integration
Competitive savings rates on share certificates (up to 3.30% APY on 9-month certificates)
cons: Limited geographic service area restricted to five Montana counties (Cascade, Pondera, Teton, Chouteau, Glacier), not available nationwide
Recent digital banking platform migration required customers to redownload apps and re-enroll with new credentials, indicating potential technical debt
Credit card rates are variable at 13.00% APR, which is higher than many competitors
No mention of fee schedules, minimum balance requirements, or overdraft protection details on their website
Limited online presence and product marketing compared to national banks or fintech competitors
best_for: Montana residents living, working, or studying in Cascade, Pondera, Teton, Chouteau, or Glacier counties seeking traditional credit union banking
Auto buyers in central Montana looking for competitive fixed-rate financing on newer vehicles
Homebuyers in the service area seeking local mortgage lending with competitive rates
Community members prioritizing member-owned, not-for-profit banking institutions over corporate banks
diagnosis: 1st Liberty is best for Montana residents within their five-county service area who value local credit union banking with competitive rates on auto loans and mortgages. The main caveat is strict geographic limitations—membership and services are only available to those with community ties to central Montana, making this unsuitable for customers outside the Cascade, Pondera, Teton, Chouteau, and Glacier counties.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
